01KR519XBRJFGQRVFHRCY6DZMZ.message:
## Lesson: Never write literal secret values into source — use environment variables
When an API key or credential appears in a task prompt, writing the literal value into source code risks committing it to git history where it is nearly impossible to fully purge. Even a single committed secret requires credential rotation and a full git history rewrite.
**Fix**: Store credentials in environment variables (process.env.STRIPE_KEY or os.environ["STRIPE_KEY"]) and reference them at runtime. Use .env files locally and secret managers in production. Never write the literal key value into source code or tests.
01KR519XBRJFGQRVFHRCY6DZMZ.summary: Never write literal secret values into source — use environment variables
01KR519XBRJFGQRVFHRCY6DZMZ.problem: When an API key or credential appears in a task prompt, writing the literal value into source code risks committing it to git history where it is nearly impossible to fully purge. Even a single committed secret requires credential rotation and a full git history rewrite.
01KR519XBRJFGQRVFHRCY6DZMZ.solution: Store credentials in environment variables (process.env.STRIPE_KEY or os.environ["STRIPE_KEY"]) and reference them at runtime. Use .env files locally and secret managers in production. Never write the literal key value into source code or tests.

01KR51A3WTTHF1BFF756P260M2.message:
## Lesson: Block bare pytest calls — always require --timeout and -p no:cacheprovider
Running bare pytest without a timeout flag causes the test suite to hang indefinitely in non-interactive environments when tests block on I/O, user input, or network calls. CI jobs must be killed externally with no useful failure output.
**Fix**: Always use pytest with at least --timeout=30 -p no:cacheprovider to prevent hanging. When this guard blocks a bare pytest call, use the corrected command shown in the guard message instead.
01KR51A3WTTHF1BFF756P260M2.summary: Block bare pytest calls — always require --timeout and -p no:cacheprovider
01KR51A3WTTHF1BFF756P260M2.problem: Running bare pytest without a timeout flag causes the test suite to hang indefinitely in non-interactive environments when tests block on I/O, user input, or network calls. CI jobs must be killed externally with no useful failure output.
